title: ArtifactExplorer Sources registry
description: Schema for schema/sources.yaml — the canonical bibliographic registry referenced by `provenance:` on artifacts, concepts, convergences, and scenarios.

id.description: Slug-like unique identifier referenced by `provenance:` entries across the corpus.

author.description: Individual, corporate, or organizational author. Required. For corporate authors use the org name as-is ('Microsoft', 'MITRE ATT&CK'). For individuals use APA last-name-first form ('Smith, R. F.').

url.description: Canonical URL. Required. Prefer the most specific page — event/technique/article — over an umbrella root.

year.description: Publication year. Optional. Use 'n.d.' for undated works per APA 7 convention.

publisher.description: Site/publisher name for APA purposes (e.g., 'Microsoft Learn', 'MITRE ATT&CK', 'libyal'). Optional. When omitted, APA rendering falls back to URL host.

accessed.description: ISO-format date the URL was last verified. Optional but recommended for web sources that may change.

doi.description: DOI for academic sources. Optional. Kept separate from url: so APA rendering can prefer the DOI when present.

apa.description: Pre-rendered APA 7 string. Optional — can be regenerated by tools/extract_sources.py or rebuilt at viewer side from the structured fields.

note.description: Editorial annotation — why this source is authoritative, known limitations, cross-references. Optional.

access-guide.description: Prose description of how to navigate the source to find content for a specific artifact. Most useful for repository-scope sources (kind: index-catalog). Should cover repository structure, URL/filename pattern, per-entry fields, and known pitfalls.

kind.description: What kind of DFIR information the source provides. Drives attribution filtering and coverage analysis.

authority.description: Authority tier. primary = format reverse-engineer / vendor doc / peer-reviewed paper. secondary = analyst writeup / community blog. tertiary = training material / summary / index.

coverage.description: What the source speaks to. Empty or missing = unclassified. Enables attribution filtering: when drafting provenance for artifact X, only sources where coverage.substrates includes X.substrate are candidates.

substrates.description: Substrates this source covers. Values must match substrate names under schema/substrate.schema.json (windows-registry, windows-evtx, windows-ntfs-metadata, etc.). Empty array = cross-substrate scope (common for behavior sources like MITRE ATT&CK).

artifacts.description: Specific artifact names the source DEDICATES or SUBSTANTIVELY covers — the source is 'about' these artifacts (dedicated section, byte-layout spec, dedicated page, multi-paragraph subsection within an umbrella). These cite-count as primary attribution sources for the named artifacts.

mentions.description: Artifact names the source references in passing (name-dropped, alluded to, cross-referenced) but does NOT substantively document. Populated during audit. Used for edge-weight computation: the fraction of an artifact's sources that mention a sister artifact measures forensic connection strength. NOT authoritative attribution — passing mentions do not constitute sourcing.

convergences.description: Convergence names the source reasons about (e.g. USED, DEVICE_CONNECTED, COMMUNICATED). Populated from Phase 5 tier-applicability. Absence is fine — many sources apply broadly across convergences (methodology, textbooks). When populated, a convergence citing this source must appear in this list (opt-in validation — relaxed vs tier-1 artifacts).

convergences-broadly.description: True if the source applies methodology-broadly across many convergences (e.g. Casey C-scale, Carvey WFA textbook). When true, `convergences` list is advisory only; any convergence may cite the source.

scenarios.description: Scenario slugs the source informs (e.g. departing-employee-usb-exfil, print-photograph-exfil-chain). Populated from Phase 5 tier-applicability. Most sources leave this empty — scenarios cite methodology sources, which declare via `scenarios-broadly`.

scenarios-broadly.description: True for foundational sources that apply to every scenario (Casey 2002/2020 evaluative opinions, Carrier FSFA methodology). When true, any scenario may cite the source without listing it in `scenarios`.

sections.description: Optional enumeration of the source's major sections. When present, `provenance[*].section` values for artifacts citing this source can be validated against this list. Only worth populating for heavily-reused sources.

sitemap-url.description: URL of a discoverable sitemap/archive/index for this source, when available. Captured during Phase 3 of the source-audit procedure. Enables programmatic re-crawl of the source's full content set.
